clusters.title: Nearly 900 arrested as PSG celebrations blighted by violence
clusters.neutral_headline: Six people stabbed in London after Arsenal’s victory parade
bullet_summary: France said on Monday that nearly 900 people had been arrested in riots that broke out after Paris Saint-Germain club won the coveted Champions League for a second consecutive year over the weekend
The Metropolitan police said the stabbings took place in the evening after most of the crowds had dispersed
The Metropolitan Police said 24 people were arrested on Sunday along or near the parade route
The interior minister, ­Laurent Nuñez, said 57 officers were wounded, with most suffering minor injuries, as football fans set off fires and vandalised shops

items.title: Nearly 900 arrested as PSG celebrations blighted by violence
items.standfirst: France said on Monday that nearly 900 people had been arrested in riots that broke out after Paris Saint-Germain club won the coveted Champions League for a second consecutive year over the weekend.Celebrations were blighted by clashes between youths and police in Paris and other cities, cars set on fire and shops looted.
items.content: France said on Monday that nearly 900 people had been arrested in riots that broke out after Paris Saint-Germain club won the coveted Champions League for a second consecutive year over the weekend.Celebrations were blighted by clashes between youths and police in Paris and other cities, cars set on fire and shops looted.

items.title: Six people stabbed in London after Arsenal’s victory parade
items.standfirst: Met says non-fatal stabbings took place after most of the crowds had dispersed on Sunday eveningSix people were stabbed after Arsenal’s Premier League victory parade in north London on Sunday, police have said.The Metropolitan police said the stabbings took place in the evening after most of the crowds had dispersed. Twenty-four people were arrested. Continue reading...
items.content: Met says non-fatal stabbings took place after most of the crowds had dispersed on Sunday eveningSix people were stabbed after Arsenal’s Premier League victory parade in north London on Sunday, police have said.The Metropolitan police said the stabbings took place in the evening after most of the crowds had dispersed. Twenty-four people were arrested. Continue reading...

items.title: Six people stabbed after Arsenal victory parade
items.standfirst: The Metropolitan Police said 24 people were arrested on Sunday along or near the parade route.
items.content: The Metropolitan Police said 24 people were arrested on Sunday along or near the parade route.

items.title: Violent clashes give way to peaceful PSG parade after Champions League win
items.standfirst: A huge crowd of supporters gathered peacefully near the Eiffel Tower on Sunday to celebrate Paris Saint-Germain's victory, which was marred by violent clashes overnight that led police to detain hundreds of people.
items.content: A huge crowd of supporters gathered peacefully near the Eiffel Tower on Sunday to celebrate Paris Saint-Germain's victory, which was marred by violent clashes overnight that led police to detain hundreds of people.(Image credit: Emma Da Silva)

items.title: Between celebration and confrontation: Paris after PSG victory
items.standfirst: Nearly 800 people were arrested after clashes with police following the Champions League final.
items.content: Nearly 800 people were arrested after clashes with police following the Champions League final.

items.title: Hundreds of people detained during celebrations of Paris Saint-German’s Champions League win
items.standfirst: French police detained 780 people across France as violent clashes in Paris and other cities erupted after Paris Saint-Germain won the Champions League title.
items.content: French police detained 780 people across France as violent clashes in Paris and other cities erupted after Paris Saint-Germain won the Champions League title.

items.title: Hundreds arrested and dozens of police injured after Champions League riots in France
items.standfirst: Nearly 800 people were arrested after clashes with police which saw 219 people injured, including 57 police officers.
items.content: Nearly 800 people were arrested after clashes with police which saw 219 people injured, including 57 police officers.

items.title: French police arrest 780 after violent clashes as PSG fans celebrate Champions League win
items.standfirst: Interior minister says 57 officers injured as rioters set fires and vandalise shops in about 15 citiesFrench police have detained 780 people involved in violent clashes in Paris and other French cities that erupted on Saturday night after Paris Saint-Germain defeated Arsenal to win the Champions League.The interior minister, ­Laurent Nuñez, said 57 officers were wounded, with most suffering minor injuries, as football fans set off fires and vandalised shops. One small group even tried to storm a Paris police station. Continue reading...
items.content: Interior minister says 57 officers injured as rioters set fires and vandalise shops in about 15 citiesFrench police have detained 780 people involved in violent clashes in Paris and other French cities that erupted on Saturday night after Paris Saint-Germain defeated Arsenal to win the Champions League.The interior minister, ­Laurent Nuñez, said 57 officers were wounded, with most suffering minor injuries, as football fans set off fires and vandalised shops. One small group even tried to storm a Paris police station. Continue reading...

items.title: Hundreds arrested across France during unrest after PSG win - as more than 50 officers injured

clusters.title: France seizes Russia-linked oil tanker
clusters.neutral_headline: France seized sanctioned Russian oil tanker with UK help, Macron says
bullet_summary: France and its partners intercepted a Russian oil tanker circumventing sanctions in the Atlantic over the weekend, President Emmanuel Macron said in a social media post on Monday
The Tagor was detained Sunday morning in international waters with the help of Britain and other partners, the French president said

items.title: France seizes Russia-linked oil tanker
items.standfirst: France's navy has intercepted a sanctioned tanker linked to the Russian oil trade in the Atlantic Ocean and ordered the vessel to head for the French mainland, in a move Russia said was illegal and bordered on "international ​piracy". French President Emmanuel ‌Macron on Monday posted a video on X showing commandos rappelling from helicopters onto the Tagor, during ⁠an operation that occurred the previous day in international waters 400 miles (740 km) west of Brittany.
items.content: France's navy has intercepted a sanctioned tanker linked to the Russian oil trade in the Atlantic Ocean and ordered the vessel to head for the French mainland, in a move Russia said was illegal and bordered on "international ​piracy". French President Emmanuel ‌Macron on Monday posted a video on X showing commandos rappelling from helicopters onto the Tagor, during ⁠an operation that occurred the previous day in international waters 400 miles (740 km) west of Brittany.

items.title: France seized sanctioned Russian oil tanker with UK help, Macron says
items.standfirst: A British helicopter provided support during the operation on Sunday, the UK ministry of defence says.
items.content: A British helicopter provided support during the operation on Sunday, the UK ministry of defence says.

items.title: French navy seizes Russia-linked oil tanker in Atlantic
items.standfirst: President Macron says ship subject to sanctions and posts video of operation that took place with UK supportA suspected Russian oil tanker has been detained in the Atlantic, France has announced, in the latest seizure aimed at combatting Moscow’s “shadow fleet” of vessels contravening international sanctions.The Tagor was detained on Sunday morning in international waters more than 400 nautical miles (740km) west of Brittany with the help of the UK and other partners, said the French president, Emmanuel Macron. Continue reading...
items.content: President Macron says ship subject to sanctions and posts video of operation that took place with UK supportA suspected Russian oil tanker has been detained in the Atlantic, France has announced, in the latest seizure aimed at combatting Moscow’s “shadow fleet” of vessels contravening international sanctions.The Tagor was detained on Sunday morning in international waters more than 400 nautical miles (740km) west of Brittany with the help of the UK and other partners, said the French president, Emmanuel Macron. Continue reading...

items.content: French Navy seizes Russian oil tanker

items.title: French navy, backed by the UK, intercepts Russian oil tanker
items.standfirst: According to France, the tanker, which had sailed from Murmansk, was trying to 'skirt international sanctions'.
items.content: According to France, the tanker, which had sailed from Murmansk, was trying to 'skirt international sanctions'.

items.title: Russian oil tanker intercepted by France and allies in Atlantic
items.standfirst: France and its partners intercepted a Russian oil tanker circumventing sanctions in the Atlantic over the weekend, President Emmanuel Macron said in a social media post on Monday. The Tagor was detained Sunday morning in international waters with the help of Britain and other partners, the French president said.
items.content: France and its partners intercepted a Russian oil tanker circumventing sanctions in the Atlantic over the weekend, President Emmanuel Macron said in a social media post on Monday. The Tagor was detained Sunday morning in international waters with the help of Britain and other partners, the French president said.

items.title: Britain helps French navy seize Russian 'shadow fleet' tanker
items.standfirst: UK forces helped the French navy intercept an oil tanker under international sanctions that was coming from Russia, alleged to be part of its shadow fleet.
items.content: UK forces helped the French navy intercept an oil tanker under international sanctions that was coming from Russia, alleged to be part of its shadow fleet.

items.title: French Navy seizes Russia-linked oil tanker in high seas, Macron says
items.standfirst: The French Navy on Sunday boarded an oil tanker, named the Tagor, which was subject to international sanctions and sailing from Russia, French President Emmanuel Macron wrote on X.
items.content: The French Navy on Sunday boarded an oil tanker, named the Tagor, which was subject to international sanctions and sailing from Russia, French President Emmanuel Macron wrote on X.

clusters.title: Runoff in high-stakes Colombian presidential election
clusters.neutral_headline: Colombia presidential runoff pits leftist senator against pro-Trump rival
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, Guardian World, NPR News and others

items.title: Runoff in high-stakes Colombian presidential election
items.standfirst: Colombian far-right candidate Abelardo de la Espriella and far-left Senator Iván Cepeda are advancing to a runoff on June 21 following a highly contested race on Sunday. María Teresa Aya with Noticias Caracol joins CBS News with more.
items.content: Colombian far-right candidate Abelardo de la Espriella and far-left Senator Iván Cepeda are advancing to a runoff on June 21 following a highly contested race on Sunday. María Teresa Aya with Noticias Caracol joins CBS News with more.

items.title: Trump admirer’s surprise first-round win is a blow to Colombia’s traditional conservatives
items.standfirst: Success of far-right presidential candidate, Abelardo de la Espriella, suggests some voters are ‘fed up with politics’The far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella and the leftwing senator Iván Cepeda have just under three weeks to compete for the roughly 3.6m votes that did not go to either of them in the first round of Colombia’s presidential election.That is no insignificant number, given that De la Espriella’s lead over Cepeda amounted to little more than 670,000 votes – 43.7% to 40.9%. Continue reading...
items.content: Success of far-right presidential candidate, Abelardo de la Espriella, suggests some voters are ‘fed up with politics’The far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella and the leftwing senator Iván Cepeda have just under three weeks to compete for the roughly 3.6m votes that did not go to either of them in the first round of Colombia’s presidential election.That is no insignificant number, given that De la Espriella’s lead over Cepeda amounted to little more than 670,000 votes – 43.7% to 40.9%. Continue reading...

items.title: Colombia’s far-right presidential candidate De la Espriella wins first round of vote ahead of runoff
items.standfirst: Lawyer and Trump admirer has risen rapidly in the polls and will face Iván Cepeda in election runoff in three weeksA far-right lawyer and Donald Trump admirer will go head-to-head against leftwing senator Iván Cepeda in the race to be Colombia’s next president after he won a surprise victory in the first round of voting.With 100% of ballots counted, the outsider Abelardo de la Espriella secured 43.7% of the vote – just over 10.3m votes – compared with 40.9% (about 9.6m votes) for Cepeda, a philosopher and human rights activist who has served as a senator since 2014 and is backed by the current leftwing president, Gustavo Petro. Continue reading...
items.content: Lawyer and Trump admirer has risen rapidly in the polls and will face Iván Cepeda in election runoff in three weeksA far-right lawyer and Donald Trump admirer will go head-to-head against leftwing senator Iván Cepeda in the race to be Colombia’s next president after he won a surprise victory in the first round of voting.With 100% of ballots counted, the outsider Abelardo de la Espriella secured 43.7% of the vote – just over 10.3m votes – compared with 40.9% (about 9.6m votes) for Cepeda, a philosopher and human rights activist who has served as a senator since 2014 and is backed by the current leftwing president, Gustavo Petro. Continue reading...

items.title: Right-wing candidate pulls ahead in first round of Colombia's presidential vote
items.standfirst: Tough-on-crime outsider Aberaldo de la Espriella took the lead in Colombia's presidential race on Sunday night, setting up a runoff with Iván Cepeda, an ally of outgoing President Gustavo Petro.
items.content: Tough-on-crime outsider Aberaldo de la Espriella took the lead in Colombia's presidential race on Sunday night, setting up a runoff with Iván Cepeda, an ally of outgoing President Gustavo Petro.(Image credit: Fernando Vergara)

items.title: Colombia’s Petro sows doubt about election as favored successor heads to runoff against pro-Trump rival
items.standfirst: Colombia’s outgoing president sowed doubt Sunday about his country’s elections, which showed his preferred candidate, Iván Cepeda, headed to a runoff next month against right-wing opponent Abelardo de la Espriella
items.content: Colombia’s outgoing president sowed doubt Sunday about his country’s elections, which showed his preferred candidate, Iván Cepeda, headed to a runoff next month against right-wing opponent Abelardo de la Espriella

items.title: Colombia presidential runoff pits leftist senator against pro-Trump rival
items.standfirst: Left-wing senator Iván Cepeda will face Trump admirer Abelardo de la Espriella at the final ballot on 21 June.
items.content: Left-wing senator Iván Cepeda will face Trump admirer Abelardo de la Espriella at the final ballot on 21 June.

items.title: Colombia goes to polls in election pitting outgoing leader’s ally against pro-Trump candidates
items.standfirst: Ballots are being cast in the first round of the South American nation’s presidential electionsColombians are casting ballots in the first round of the South American nation’s presidential election, choosing between candidates with radically diverging visions for the future of peace in a country haunted by decades of armed conflict.The vote on Sunday, seen as a referendum on outgoing President Gustavo Petro’s policies, comes 10 years after Colombia signed a historic peace pact with guerrillas of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Farc). Continue reading...
items.content: Ballots are being cast in the first round of the South American nation’s presidential electionsColombians are casting ballots in the first round of the South American nation’s presidential election, choosing between candidates with radically diverging visions for the future of peace in a country haunted by decades of armed conflict.The vote on Sunday, seen as a referendum on outgoing President Gustavo Petro’s policies, comes 10 years after Colombia signed a historic peace pact with guerrillas of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Farc). Continue reading...

items.title: US says it struck Iranian radar sites as Iran targets American forces in Kuwait
items.standfirst: Iran and the US say they have carried out fresh strikes around the Strait of Hormuz, with Kuwait condemning "repeated" Iranian attacks.
items.content: Iran and the US say they have carried out fresh strikes around the Strait of Hormuz, with Kuwait condemning "repeated" Iranian attacks.

items.title: Kuwait intercepts drones, missiles as US and Iran trade fire
items.standfirst: Kuwait's air defences intercepted missile and drone attacks, the military said Monday, after the US said it had struck Iranian military sites at the weekend and Iran's Revolutionary Guards said it had targeted a US base in response.
items.content: Kuwait's air defences intercepted missile and drone attacks, the military said Monday, after the US said it had struck Iranian military sites at the weekend and Iran's Revolutionary Guards said it had targeted a US base in response.

items.title: Kuwait condemns Iranian attack as Iran-US trade new strikes
items.standfirst: Kuwait activated its air defences after what its foreign ministry later described as a ‘heinous Iranian attack’.
items.content: Kuwait activated its air defences after what its foreign ministry later described as a ‘heinous Iranian attack’.

items.title: Iran’s IRGC launches retaliatory strike after US attacks
items.standfirst: Kuwait, which hosts US forces, says its defence systems intercepted missiles and drones amid sirens across the country.
items.content: Kuwait, which hosts US forces, says its defence systems intercepted missiles and drones amid sirens across the country.

items.title: U.S. bombs Iranian military sites and Kuwait is hit by drone and missile fire
items.standfirst: The U.S. military says it bombed Iranian radar and drone control sites in Iran after Tehran shot down an American MQ-1 Predator drone this weekend. Kuwait said its air defenses opened fire on Monday.
items.content: The U.S. military says it bombed Iranian radar and drone control sites in Iran after Tehran shot down an American MQ-1 Predator drone this weekend. Kuwait said its air defenses opened fire on Monday. (Image credit: AP Photo)

items.title: U.S. and Iran trade strikes amid talks to end war
items.standfirst: The U.S. military said it carried out what it called self-defense strikes on Iranian radar and drone control sites over the weekend, while Iran said it targeted an air base used in the U.S. attack as the two countries continued to trade strikes amid talks to end their war
items.content: The U.S. military said it carried out what it called self-defense strikes on Iranian radar and drone control sites over the weekend, while Iran said it targeted an air base used in the U.S. attack as the two countries continued to trade strikes amid talks to end their war

items.title: Live: Israeli military issues evacuation warning for Beirut's southern suburbs
Israel PM orders strikes on Beirut suburbs as Hezbollah conflict escalates
items.standfirst: Benjamin Netanyahu says the Hezbollah stronghold of Dahieh will be targeted in response to attacks on Israeli civilians.
items.content: Benjamin Netanyahu says the Hezbollah stronghold of Dahieh will be targeted in response to attacks on Israeli civilians.

items.title: 'Chaotic scenes' in Beirut after Netanyahu orders strikes
items.standfirst: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Monday ordered ​the military to attack targets in the Lebanese capital Beirut's southern suburbs, a Hezbollah stronghold known as Dahiyeh, prompting residents to scramble to flee the area. France 24's correspondent in Beirut, Renée Davis, has the latest.
items.content: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Monday ordered ​the military to attack targets in the Lebanese capital Beirut's southern suburbs, a Hezbollah stronghold known as Dahiyeh, prompting residents to scramble to flee the area. items.content: Videos show gridlocked traffic in the southern suburbs of Beirut as residents rushed to evacuate the Dahiyeh area.

items.title: Netanyahu orders Israeli bombing of southern Beirut
items.standfirst: Thousands leave homes after Israeli military instructed to strike ‘terrorist targets’ in largest escalation of war since ceasefire• Middle East crisis – live updatesBenjamin Netanyahu has instructed the Israeli military to bomb the southern suburbs of Beirut, the most serious escalation of Israel’s war in Lebanon since a supposed ceasefire was announced on 17 April.The Israeli prime minister and his defence minister, Israel Katz, said on Monday they had given instructions to strike “terrorist targets” in the southern suburbs for what they called “repeated and ongoing violations of the ceasefire by Hezbollah”. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Ethiopia votes in elections expected to give Abiy's party a landslide win
clusters.neutral_headline: Voting suspended in parts of Ethiopia over security concerns
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, BBC World, Al Jazeera

items.title: Ethiopia votes in elections expected to give Abiy's party a landslide win
items.standfirst: Ethiopians voted on Monday in parliamentary and regional elections expected to hand Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed’s ruling Prosperity Party a landslide win. There was no election in the Tigray region due to “unfavourable conditions”, and voting in the Oromo and Amhara regions were interrupted due to security problems, according to election officials.
items.content: Ethiopians voted on Monday in parliamentary and regional elections expected to hand Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed’s ruling Prosperity Party a landslide win. There was no election in the Tigray region due to “unfavourable conditions”, and voting in the Oromo and Amhara regions were interrupted due to security problems, according to election officials.

items.title: Voting suspended in parts of Ethiopia over security concerns
items.standfirst: Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed's party is expected to dominate the election as conflicts overshadow the vote.
items.content: Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed's party is expected to dominate the election as conflicts overshadow the vote.

items.title: Ethiopia holds elections with PM Abiy’s party expected to dominate
items.standfirst: Voting will not take place in northern Tigray region and some parts of the Amhara region amid insecurity.
items.content: Voting will not take place in northern Tigray region and some parts of the Amhara region amid insecurity.

items.title: WHO calls for community cooperation to contain Ebola outbreak in DRC
items.standfirst: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus makes appeal after protests against protocols for handling victims’ bodies in Ituri provinceContaining the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo requires community cooperation and is “everybody’s business”, the World Health Organization has said.Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, the organisation’s director general, made the plea on Sunday during a visit to eastern Congo where some residents have protested against stringent medical protocols for handling victims’ bodies. Continue reading...

items.content: The World Health Organisation (WHO) chief has visited the province hardest hit by the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), urging residents to seek treatment and practice safe burials.

clusters.title: Hungary’s Magyar threatens constitutional amendment if Orban-appointed president refuses to resign
clusters.neutral_headline: Hungary’s Magyar to amend the constitution to remove President Tamas Sulyok

items.title: Hungary’s Magyar threatens constitutional amendment if Orban-appointed president refuses to resign
items.standfirst: Hungarian Prime Minister Peter Magyar on Monday threatened legal action and said the government would use its two-thirds parliamentary majority to amend the constitution to ​force President Tamas Sulyok from office. Sulyok, who was appointed under former prime minister Viktor Orban, has refused to resign before his term ends in 2029.
items.content: Hungarian Prime Minister Peter Magyar on Monday threatened legal action and said the government would use its two-thirds parliamentary majority to amend the constitution to ​force President Tamas Sulyok from office. Sulyok, who was appointed under former prime minister Viktor Orban, has refused to resign before his term ends in 2029.

items.title: Hungary’s Magyar to amend the constitution to remove President Tamas Sulyok
items.standfirst: After coming to power in April, PM Peter Magyar had given Sulyok a deadline of Sunday to leave office.
items.content: After coming to power in April, PM Peter Magyar had given Sulyok a deadline of Sunday to leave office.
